trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at score 60.30 (0.95ms kernel vs 0.98ms reference). Profiling reveals GEMM is 97.5% of GPU time, elementwise only 2.5%. Advancing to optimization stage where the focus must be on fusing matmul with the epilogue.

nudges.text: Your profiling showed GEMM is 97.5% of time, yet you keep optimizing the 2.5% elementwise kernel. Consider trying `torch.compile(mode="default")` on the forward method to let the compiler fuse the matmul epilogue with the mish activations — this is the highest-leverage first optimization to try. Alternatively, write a Triton matmul kernel with fused bias+mish epilogue to eliminate the separate kernel launch and memory round-trip for the 8192x8192 output.

The `torch.compile` failed because you wrapped it around your custom Triton kernel. Instead, try a simpler approach: make `ModelNew.forward` just do `x = self.linear(x); x = F.mish(x); x = F.mish(x); return x` (pure PyTorch ops, no custom Triton kernel) and apply `torch.compile(mode="default")` to the forward method. This lets the compiler fuse the GEMM epilogue with the activations, which is where the real speedup opportunity is.
